mnemonics: IoT Devices Are Constantly In-Touch (CIT) - Continuous Internal Testing
keyPoints: Continuous Internal Evaluation refers to the ongoing, automated self-assessment and health-checking performed by an IoT device itself.
It involves monitoring key parameters like power levels, memory usage, sensor accuracy, and network connectivity to ensure operational integrity.
The goal is to predict failures, perform preventative maintenance, and trigger alerts without always requiring external intervention.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with external monitoring done by a central platform or gateway.
Overlooking the importance of designing devices with the necessary hardware (e.g., self-diagnostic sensors) to perform these evaluations.
